exercises.title: Basic while Loop with Counter
exercises.description: A while loop needs a counter that eventually makes the condition False. Print numbers from 1 to 5 using a while loop.
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Use a while loop to print numbers 1 through 5
count = 1

while count <= 5:
    print(count)
    count += 1
hints: Increment count inside the loop with count += 1 to avoid an infinite loop.

exercises.title: Accumulator Pattern
exercises.description: While loops are useful for accumulating totals. Sum all integers from 1 to 10 using a while loop.
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Sum integers from 1 to 10 using a while loop
total = 0
num = 1

while num <= 10:
    total += num
    num += 1

print(f"Sum of 1 to 10: {total}")
hints: total += num adds the current number to the running total.

exercises.title: Countdown with Conditional Messages
exercises.description: A countdown that prints special messages at specific points. Print 5 down to 1 with a special message at 3.
hints: Combine a while loop with an if statement inside the body.
countdown -= 1 decrements the counter each iteration.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Three Parts of a Safe while Loop
warmup.description: Initialize, condition, update. Without the update, the loop runs forever.
warmup.starterCode:
# 1. Initialize
power = 1
# 2. Condition
while power <= 100:
    print(power)
    # 3. Update
    power *= 2
